@@ -1537,7 +1537,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.2","title":"Docs: README/quickstart + architecture + CLI/robot/MCP reference","description":"# Task: Documentation for v0.1

## Goal
Make the project approachable for a new contributor and operable for users.

This bead is intentionally self-contained and mirrors PLAN Appendix A + F so contributors don’t need to dig through planning docs.

## Deliverables
### 1) README (operator-first)
- What wa is (WezTerm mux “terminal hypervisor” for agent fleets).
- Quickstart:
  - install wa
  - `wa setup`
  - `wa watch`
  - `wa status`
  - `wa query` / `wa events`
- Safety guarantees:
  - observe vs act split
  - no silent gaps
  - policy-gated sending + approvals

### 2) Architecture doc
- Ingest → Storage (SQLite+FTS5) → Pattern engine → Workflow engine → Robot/MCP.
- Deterministic state via OSC 133.
- Explicit GAP semantics.

### 3) CLI reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Human-oriented commands (rich output by default via `rich_rust`):
- `wa status` — panes + inferred agent state
- `wa watch` — run daemon (foreground/background)
- `wa stop` — stop watcher in workspace safely
- `wa events` — recent/unhandled events
- `wa query` — FTS search
- `wa send` — guarded send to a pane
- `wa reserve` — reserve a pane for exclusive use
- `wa reservations` — list active reservations
- `wa approve` — grant allow-once approvals
- `wa workflow` — run a workflow manually (guarded)
- `wa audit` — audit trail of actions (redacted)
- `wa history` — action timeline + undoability
- `wa undo` — undo supported actions (with confirmation)
- `wa rules` — list/test rule packs, show matching traces
- `wa accounts` — list/refresh accounts, show rotation picks
- `wa setup` — canonical WezTerm configuration (local/remote)
- `wa doctor` — environment checks (wezterm presence, DB health)
- `wa diag bundle` — sanitized diagnostic bundle export
- `wa export` — export slices/history/audit
- `wa web` — optional HTTP server (`fastapi_rust`) if enabled
- `wa tui` — optional TUI (`charmed_rust`) if enabled
- `wa sync` — optional sync (`asupersync`) if enabled

Robot mode commands (stable JSON envelope; token-efficient):
- `wa robot state [--domain <name>] [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot get-text <pane_id> [--tail N] [--escapes]`
- `wa robot send <pane_id> "<text>" [--no-newline] [--wait-for "<pat>"] [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot wait-for <pane_id> "<pat>" [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot search "<fts query>" [--pane-id <id>] [--since <iso8601>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot events [--unhandled] [--pane-id <id>] [--type <event>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id> [--force]`
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations`
- `wa robot accounts [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot accounts refresh [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot rules list [--pack <name>]`
- `wa robot rules test "<text>" [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot quick-start`

### 4) MCP reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Tools (names are short and obvious):
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- `wa.reserve`
- `wa.release`
- `wa.reservations`

Resources:
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- `wa://reservations`

### 5) Library integration map (PLAN Appendix F)
| Library | Role in `wa` |
|---------|--------------|
| `cass` (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search`) | Correlation + session archaeology; used in status + workflows |
| `caut` (`/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker`) | Usage truth + selection; used in accounts + `handle_usage_limits` |
| `rich_rust` | Human-first CLI output (tables/panels/highlight) |
| `charmed_rust` | Optional interactive TUI (pane picker, event feed, transcript viewer) |
| `fastmcp_rust` | MCP tool surface for agent control (mirrors robot mode) |
| `fastapi_rust` | Optional HTTP server for dashboards/webhooks (read-only first) |
| `asupersync` | Remote bootstrap/sync layer (configs, binaries, DB snapshots) |
| `playwright` | Automate device auth flows with persistent profiles |
| `ast-grep` | Structure-aware codebase scans in “unstick agent” workflows |

## Testing / doc correctness
Docs must not drift.

- The quickstart commands shown in README should be validated regularly:
  - either by referencing existing E2E scripts, or
  - by adding a small “docs smoke” CI step that runs the exact commands from the docs in a controlled environment (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

- Robot mode docs should match the schema contract:
  - reference the versioned JSON schemas and keep field names consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new engineer can get to a running watcher and see events with no oral tradition.
- CLI/robot/MCP references match implemented command surfaces and schemas.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.20","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] Capture current green CI quality-gate evidence","description":"## Scope
Capture a current successful GitHub Actions CI run for the ship-readiness gate after HEAD 8759ac8c.

## Evidence from ft-v5lz3.4 audit
- CI workflow is configured to run fmt, clippy -D warnings, tests, feature/runtime matrices, build artifacts, version smoke, and E2E artifact upload.

- If the run is red, file concrete child beads for failing jobs instead of closing this.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:18.407793Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:36.874986Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:36.874615Z","close_reason":"Duplicate evidence bead created during audit; superseded by wa-nu4.3.9.22 and wa-nu4.3.9.23.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.20","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:18.407793Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.21","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] Release closure proof: verify release workflow artifacts and smoke output","description":"## Gap\nwa-nu4.3.9 requires the release workflow/versioning to produce reproducible binaries with build metadata and release smoke tests. The audit verified release.yml wiring, SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH support, checksums, and attestations, but gh release view returned 'release not found', git tag --list 'v*' was empty, and gh run list --workflow=Release returned no runs. There is no live release-run artifact evidence yet.\n\n## Deliverable\n1. Trigger or inspect the intended v0.1.0 release workflow run when the operator has chosen the tag.\n2. Record the Release run id, tag, target matrix result, uploaded assets, SHA256SUMS, and attestation step result.\n3. Verify at least one downloaded Unix artifact runs ft --version, ft version --full, ft --help, and ft doctor --json, matching release.yml's smoke contract.\n4. If publishing is intentionally deferred, update wa-nu4.3.9 notes with the exact blocker/decision and keep the epic open.\n\n## Acceptance\n- Release evidence exists from a real Release workflow run or explicit operator blocker.\n- Artifact list includes Linux and Apple Silicon targets at minimum.\n- Version/build metadata smoke output is cited.\n- No implementation drift is hidden under this evidence bead.","notes":"Release proof check completed by cod_4 on 2026-04-28. Workflow is active (gh workflow list shows Release active) and release.yml recipe is wired: matrix assets at lines 33-53, SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0 at lines 66-69, binary smoke tests at lines 71-110, archive/checksum creation at lines 112-129, upload-artifact at lines 131-134, SHA256SUMS at lines 167-174, attestations over archives/checksums/SHA256SUMS at lines 176-188, release publish at lines 190-197. Blocking condition: no approved release tag/artifact source exists. Evidence: gh run list --workflow=release.yml --limit 20 returned []; gh release view returned 'release not found'; gh release list --limit 20 --json ... returned []; git tag --list 'v*' and git ls-remote --tags origin 'refs/tags/v*' returned no tags. Therefore there are no release workflow artifacts or checksums to verify without triggering a release, which this bead does not authorize. wa-nu4.3.9.25 remains the future tag/run artifact proof bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:22.373629Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:16:45.482553Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:16:45.482197Z","close_reason":"Blocked surfaced: release.yml recipe is wired, but no v* tag, Release workflow run, GitHub release, artifacts, or checksums exist to verify; future release-run proof remains wa-nu4.3.9.25","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distribution","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.21","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:22.373629Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

As of audit HEAD 8759ac8c, gh run list --workflow=CI --limit 5 showed the latest CI run queued (databaseId 25021655622, head 051f3b67) and recent prior CI runs cancelled, so the audit could verify workflow wiring but not a green current run.\n\n## Deliverable\n1. Inspect the latest main CI run with gh run list --workflow=CI --limit 5 --json databaseId,status,conclusion,headSha,createdAt,displayTitle.\n2. If a current run is green, append the run id/headSha/conclusion evidence to wa-nu4.3.9 and close this bead.\n3. If the run fails, file or fix the smallest concrete blocker bead and leave wa-nu4.3.9 open.\n4. Confirm E2E/artifact upload jobs are present in the run summary, not only in YAML.\n\n## Acceptance\n- A successful CI run on main is cited by databaseId, headSha, and conclusion=success.\n- Any failure is decomposed into specific follow-up beads rather than blocking the epic anonymously.\n- Scope stays to evidence collection plus one tiny blocker fix if obvious.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:22.417033Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:22.417033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.22","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:22.417033Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.23","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] CI: get latest ci.yml quality gate green and attach failure artifacts","description":"## Gap
The ship-readiness epic requires cargo fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test, and E2E to pass in CI. The workflow wiring exists, but live GitHub evidence is not green: gh run view 25021655622 shows the latest ci.yml run for HEAD 051f3b67 is still status=queued overall, with Lint (macos-14) failed at Check formatting and Test (macos-14) failed at Run tests; many other jobs remain queued. gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --status=success --limit 3 returned [].

## Scope
1. Inspect the completed logs/artifacts for run 25021655622 once GitHub exposes them.
2. Fix the concrete fmt/test failures if they are repo issues and fit in one hour; otherwise file exact child bugs with log excerpts.
3. Re-run ci.yml on main and capture a successful run URL, or document the remaining runner/infra blocker.

## Acceptance
- A ci.yml run at or after 051f3b67 completes successfully, or every non-green job has a linked concrete bug bead with file/log evidence.
- The bead close reason includes gh run URLs and job names.
- E2E artifact upload is confirmed from the completed run artifacts when failures occur.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:53.001607Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:13:39.401493Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","ship-readiness","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.23","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:53.001607Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.24","title":"CI: record a non-cancelled successful full CI run for ship-readiness","description":"# Purpose
Prove wa-nu4.3.9 CI acceptance with a completed successful `CI` workflow run on `main`, not just static YAML inspection.

# Evidence from ft-v5lz3.4 audit
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` statically defines fmt/clippy/test/e2e/artifact gates.
- `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --limit 5 --json ...` on 2026-04-28 showed latest run queued (`25021655622`) and recent older runs cancelled.
- `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --limit 50 ... | jq '[.[] | select(.conclusion=="success")]'` returned `[]`.

# Scope
1. Wait for or trigger one current `CI` workflow run on `main`.
2. If it succeeds, update this bead with the run URL, head SHA, and a JSON predicate showing `conclusion == "success"`.
3. If it fails or stays queued/cancelled, file or fix the concrete blocker rather than marking wa-nu4.3.9 complete.

# Acceptance
- A non-cancelled `CI` run on `main` has `status=completed` and `conclusion=success`.
- The close reason includes the `gh run view <id> --json status,conclusion,url,headSha` evidence.
- Any failure is decomposed into a concrete bug/task bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:02.059049Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:06:27.222656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:06:27.222343Z","close_reason":"Superseded by existing CI proof/failure children wa-nu4.3.9.22 and wa-nu4.3.9.23 from the same ft-v5lz3.4 audit.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.24","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:02.059049Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.25","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] Release proof: verify tag workflow assets, metadata, checksums, provenance, reproducibility","description":"## Gap
The release workflow is wired, but there is no live release proof to close wa-nu4.3.9: gh run list --workflow=release.yml --limit 5 returned [] and gh release view returned 'release not found'. release.yml builds target assets, sets SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0, runs ft --version / ft version --full / ft --help / ft doctor --json smoke tests, publishes SHA256SUMS, and generates attestations, but no tag/workflow run artifacts were available to verify.

## Scope
1. Run the release workflow on the v0.1.0 candidate tag or an approved release-candidate tag.
2. Verify downloadable assets exist for the release matrix and have matching SHA256SUMS.
3. Run at least one downloaded Unix binary and one macOS binary with ft --version and ft version --full; record build metadata output.
4. Prove reproducibility for one target by building the same commit twice with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0 and comparing hashes, or file a narrower bug if that proof fails.
5. Confirm provenance/attestation artifacts exist for archives and checksums.

## Acceptance
- Close reason includes the release run URL, release URL, checked asset names, version output snippets, and hash/provenance evidence.
- If no release should be cut yet, leave the bead open with the explicit blocking condition rather than closing the parent epic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:03.801007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:03.801007Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["release","reproducibility","ship-readiness","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.25","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:03.801007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
